items.heading: Analysis
items.description: Gather all requirements and define the scope by collaborating with stakeholders and identifying the most critical features required for the MVP launch.

items.heading: Design
items.description: Create high-fidelity wireframes and mockups for the feature layout, ensuring that accessibility standards are met and the design system is strictly followed.

items.heading: Development
items.description: Write clean, modular code to implement the necessary logic, using Test-Driven Development (TDD) to prevent regressions over time.

items.heading: Testing
items.description: Verify the functionality through automated UI tests and user acceptance testing (UAT) to guarantee stable behavior in production edge cases.

items.heading: Phase 1: Foundation
items.description: Set up the repository, configure linters and formatters, and establish the CI/CD pipelines to ensure swift future deployments.

items.heading: Phase 2: Database
items.description: Draft the initial database schemas, generate Prisma migrations, and seed the development environments with realistic, high volume mock data.

items.heading: Phase 3: Logic
items.description: Construct RESTful API routes, validation schemas using Zod, and robust error handlers that provide actionable feedback to front-end clients.

items.heading: Frontend
items.description: React and TailwindCSS implementation.

items.heading: Backend
items.description: NodeJS and Express server.

items.heading: Database
items.description: PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM.

items.heading: DevOps
items.description: Docker and GitHub Actions deployment.

items.heading: Step 1
items.description: Identify the core problem.

items.heading: Step 2
items.description: Brainstorm potential solutions.

items.heading: Step 3
items.description: Select the best approach.

items.heading: Step 4
items.description: Execute the chosen plan.

items.heading: Start
items.description: Begin the journey by establishing a strong foundation and aligning all team members on the core objectives. A solid start prevents future bottlenecks.

items.heading: Middle
items.description: Overcome the challenges through iterative development and constant feedback loops. This is where the bulk of the engineering and design work occurs, requiring high collaboration.

items.heading: End
items.description: Achieve the final goal with a rigorous QA cycle and staged rollout. Celebrate the milestones while preparing precise documentation for future handoffs.
